Storm.

Conscious Hip-Hop Artist • Storyteller • Lyricist

A rap artist from St. Louis, Missouri — writing since the age of twelve, shaped between his hometown and a military base in Germany. Raw stories, sharp lyricism, and a mission to make his mark on underground hip-hop.

🎤 About the Artist

From St. Louis
To The Underground

Born K Carlton Allen, Kalm Before the Storm is a rap artist from St. Louis, Missouri.

He began writing rap lyrics at the age of twelve, splitting time between his hometown and the military base in Germany where his Air Force veteran father was stationed.

His musical influences include Nas, Guru (Gang Starr), Ice Cube, Lupe Fiasco, and Common.

His music focuses on themes including Black nationalism, military life, mental disorders, and street stories.

His goal is to make a significant impact within the underground hip-hop scene.

🎧

The Sound

Rooted in the lyricism of Nas, Guru of Gang Starr, Ice Cube, Lupe Fiasco, and Common — conscious hip-hop built on real storytelling.

📝

The Themes

His music explores Black nationalism, military life, mental disorders, and street stories — lived experience turned into bars.

🎯

The Mission

To make a significant impact within the underground hip-hop scene, one record at a time.

Formative Years

Between Two Worlds
